heading: Limitation on establishment or operation of diplomatic intelligence support centers
label: 3052 Limitation on establishment or operation of diplomatic intelligence support centers

children.text: A diplomatic intelligence support center may not be established, operated, or maintained without the prior approval of the Director of National Intelligence.

The Director may only approve the establishment, operation, or maintenance of a diplomatic intelligence support center if the Director determines that the establishment, operation, or maintenance of such center is required to provide necessary intelligence support in furtherance of the national security interests of the United States.

children.heading: Prohibition of use of appropriations
children.label: b Prohibition of use of appropriations
children.text: Amounts appropriated pursuant to authorizations by law for intelligence and intelligence-related activities may not be obligated or expended for the establishment, operation, or maintenance of a diplomatic intelligence support center that is not approved by the Director of National Intelligence.

children.label: A
children.text: consists of intelligence analyses on military or political matters and expertise to conduct limited assessments and dynamic taskings for a chief of mission; and

children.label: B
children.text: is not intelligence support traditionally provided to a chief of mission by the Director of National Intelligence.

The term “chief of mission” has the meaning given that term by , and includes ambassadors at large and ministers of diplomatic missions of the United States, or persons appointed to lead United States offices abroad designated by the Secretary of State as diplomatic in nature.section 3902(3) of title 22

children.heading: Termination
children.label: d Termination
children.text: This section shall cease to be effective on .October 1, 2000
